text: This verse is generally understood to mean, If crucifixion is the lot of the innocent, what punishment is to be expected by the guilty (that is, the Jews)?

See Mt. 27.54; Mk. 15.39. St Augustine suggests that the centurion did not recognize in our Lord the unique Son of God, but only ‘a son of God’ in the general sense in which ‘a just man’ would be the equivalent of that term; cf. Wis. 2.16. But it is possible that ‘the Just One’ was used in very early times as a cipher-word for ‘Son of God’; cf. Ac. 3.14; 7.52; 22.14.
